{{< include /_macros.qmd >}}

# Tokenization {#sec-tokenization}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## In this chapter
<!-- The unglamorous, consequential first decision: how raw text becomes a
     sequence of integers. Why the choice leaks into everything downstream. -->
:::

**Learning objectives.**

<!-- TODO -->
- [ ]
- [ ]
- [ ]

## The vocabulary problem

<!-- Characters vs. words vs. subwords. Open vocabulary, rare words, the
     trade-off between sequence length and vocabulary size. -->

## Bytes as the substrate

<!-- Why byte-level is a principled floor: no unknown tokens, language-
     agnostic, Unicode/UTF-8 realities. -->

## Byte-pair encoding

<!-- The BPE algorithm: iterative merging of frequent pairs. Worked small
     example. Reference: `src/thebook/tokenizer.py`. -->

## Alternatives

<!-- Unigram / SentencePiece, WordPiece; a word on tokenizer-free ideas. -->

## Consequences

<!-- Tokenization artifacts: numbers, whitespace, code, multilingual bias;
     how the vocab interacts with the embedding matrix (@sec-embeddings). -->

## Exercises {.unnumbered}

<!-- TODO: implement BPE train/encode/decode; measure compression ratio. -->

## Further reading {.unnumbered}
